meta.originalTitle: What Lucy Taught Us 露西化石

children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1–13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.
A scientific finding in east Africa has changed our understanding of how humans have developed

On a Sunday morning in late November 1974, a team of scientists were digging in an isolated spot in the Afar region of Ethiopia. Surveying the area, palaeoanthropologist Donald Johanson spotted a small piece of bone. Straight away, he recognised that it came from the elbow of a human ancestor. And there were plenty more. “As I looked up the slopes to my left, I saw bits of the skull, a chunk of jaw, a couple of vertebrae,” says Johanson.

It was immediately obvious that the skeleton was a significant find, because the sediments at the site were known to be 3.5 million years old. “I realised this was part of a skeleton that was older than three million years,” says Johanson. It was the most ancient early human ever found. Later it became apparent that it was also the most complete – 40% of the skeleton had been preserved.

At the group's campsite that night, Johanson played a Beatles song called “Lucy in the Sky with Diamonds", and, as the feeling was that the skeleton was female due to its size, someone suggested calling it Lucy. The name stuck and Johanson says, “All of a sudden, she became a person. But the morning after the discovery, the discussion was dominated by questions. How old was Lucy when she died? Did she have children? And might she be our direct ancestor?” Nowadays, we're starting to get the answers to some of these questions.

According to Johanson, Lucy had an incredible combination of primitive and derived features, which had not been seen before. Her skull and jaws were more ape-like than those of other groups of early humans. Her braincase was also very small, no bigger than that of a chimpanzee.

For Johanson, it was immediately apparent that Lucy walked upright. That's because the shape and positioning of her pelvis reflected a fully upright gait. Lucy's knee and ankle were also preserved and seemed to reflect bipedal walking. Later studies of feet offer even more evidence. As an upright walker, Lucy strengthened the idea that walking was one of the selective pressures driving human evolution forwards. Early humans did not need bigger brains to take defining steps away from apes. Extra brainpower only came over a million years later with the arrival of the species Homo erectus, meaning upright man. Though big brains would clearly be important later, walking remains one of the traits that makes us uniquely human.

She may have walked like a human, but Lucy spent at least some of her time up in the trees, as chimpanzees and orangutans still do today. It may be that upright walking evolved in the trees, as a way to walk along branches that would otherwise be too flexible. It's not clear why Lucy left the safety of the trees. It is thought that savannahs were gradually opening up, so trees were spaced further apart. But hunting for food may have been the real reason for heading to the ground, says Chris Stringer of the Natural History Museum in London. In line with this idea, recent evidence suggests that the diet of early humans was changing at that time.

Studies of the remains of food trapped on preserved human teeth indicate that several species, including Lucy's, were expanding their diet around 3.5 million years ago. Instead of mostly eating fruit from trees, they began to include grasses and possibly meat. This change in diet may have allowed them to range more widely, and to travel around more efficiently in a changing environment. Fossilised crocodile and turtle eggs were found near her skeleton, suggesting that Lucy died while foraging for them in a nearby lake.

How did early humans process all these new foods? Later species, like Homo erectus, are known to have used simple stone tools, but no tools have ever been found from this far back. However, in 2010 archaeologists uncovered animal bones with scratches that seem to have been made by stone tools. This suggests that Lucy and her relatives used stone tools to eat meat. There have since been heated debates over whether or not the marks were really made by tools. But if they were, it is not surprising, says Fred Spoor of the Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology in Leipzig, Germany.

It also seems that Lucy's childhood was much briefer than ours and that she had to fend for herself from a young age. We know that Lucy was a full-grown adult because she had wisdom teeth and her bones had fused. But unlike modern humans, she seems to have grown to full size very quickly, and the time of death was when she was around 12 years old. In line with that, a recent study of a 3-year-old early human suggested that their brains matured much earlier than ours do.
Do the following statements agree with the information in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 1–5 on your answer sheet, write:
if the statement agrees with the information
if the statement contradicts the information
if there is no information about this
Donald Johanson was uncertain about the nature of the elbow bone he found in Afar.
Several bones were found by Donald Johanson at the same site in Afar.
The experts realised the importance of the discovery at Afar.
It was the upper part of the skeleton that had suffered the least damage.
The skeleton's measurements helped Johanson's team to decide if it was male or female.

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：唐纳德・约翰森不确定在阿法尔地区发现的肘部骨骼的性质。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Straight away, he recognised that it came from the elbow of a human ancestor”，“straight away recognised” 对应 “uncertain” 的反义，明确提到约翰森立刻认出骨骼性质，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：唐纳德・约翰森在阿法尔地区的同一地点发现了多块骨骼。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “As I looked up the slopes to my left, I saw bits of the skull, a chunk of jaw, a couple of vertebrae”，明确提到在同一地点发现头骨、下颌骨、椎骨等多块骨骼，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：专家们意识到在阿法尔地区发现的重要性。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “It was immediately obvious that the skeleton was a significant find, because the sediments at the site were known to be 3.5 million years old”，“immediately obvious”“significant find” 对应 “realised the importance”，明确提到专家立刻意识到发现的重要性，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：骨骼的上半身受损最少。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 2 仅提到 “40% 的骨骼得以保留”，Paragraph 5 提到 “骨盆、膝盖、脚踝、足部骨骼保留”，未比较 “上半身与其他部位的受损程度”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：骨骼尺寸帮助约翰森团队判断其性别。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “as the feeling was that the skeleton was female due to its size, someone suggested calling it Lucy”，“due to its size” 对应 “measurements helped decide”，明确提到通过尺寸判断为雌性，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

items.text:
（1）题目 1：Donald Johanson was uncertain about the nature of the elbow bone he found in Afar.
题目翻译：唐纳德・约翰森不确定在阿法尔地区发现的肘部骨骼的性质。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Straight away, he recognised that it came from the elbow of a human ancestor”，“straight away recognised” 对应 “uncertain” 的反义，明确提到约翰森立刻认出骨骼性质，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（2）题目 2：Several bones were found by Donald Johanson at the same site in Afar.
题目翻译：唐纳德・约翰森在阿法尔地区的同一地点发现了多块骨骼。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “As I looked up the slopes to my left, I saw bits of the skull, a chunk of jaw, a couple of vertebrae”，明确提到在同一地点发现头骨、下颌骨、椎骨等多块骨骼，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（3）题目 3：The experts realised the importance of the discovery at Afar.
题目翻译：专家们意识到在阿法尔地区发现的重要性。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “It was immediately obvious that the skeleton was a significant find, because the sediments at the site were known to be 3.5 million years old”，“immediately obvious”“significant find” 对应 “realised the importance”，明确提到专家立刻意识到发现的重要性，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（4）题目 4：It was the upper part of the skeleton that had suffered the least damage.
题目翻译：骨骼的上半身受损最少。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 2 仅提到 “40% 的骨骼得以保留”，Paragraph 5 提到 “骨盆、膝盖、脚踝、足部骨骼保留”，未比较 “上半身与其他部位的受损程度”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（5）题目 5：The skeleton’s measurements helped Johanson’s team to decide if it was male or female.
题目翻译：骨骼尺寸帮助约翰森团队判断其性别。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “as the feeling was that the skeleton was female due to its size, someone suggested calling it Lucy”，“due to its size” 对应 “measurements helped decide”，明确提到通过尺寸判断为雌性，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

children.text: from the passage for each answer.
jaws and skull like those of an ape
braincase similar in size to that of a chimp
the positioning and shape of her pelvis made it clear that she walked like a human
upright movement possibly started among the
of the skeletons of early humans shows changes in their diet
it is likely that meat and grasses were substituted for
that were located close to Lucy suggest these were also part of her diet
that were found had marks on them, possibly made by tools used for eating
of modern-day humans appear to develop later than Lucy's did

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：移动方式 - 直立行走可能起源于树的_\___\___之间
答案：branches（树枝）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “It may be that upright walking evolved in the trees, as a way to walk along branches that would otherwise be too flexible”，明确提到直立行走可能起源于在树枝间行走，因此答案为 “branches”。
题目翻译：移动方式 - 可能为寻找食物走向_\___\___
答案：ground（地面）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “hunting for food may have been the real reason for heading to the ground”，明确提到为寻找食物走向地面，因此答案为 “ground”。
题目翻译：饮食习性 - 对早期人类骨骼_\___\___中食物的分析显示其饮食变化
答案：teeth（牙齿）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “Studies of the remains of food trapped on preserved human teeth indicate that several species, including Lucy’s, were expanding their diet”，明确提到分析牙齿上的食物残留，因此答案为 “teeth”。
题目翻译：饮食习性 - 肉类和草类可能取代了_\___\___
答案：fruit（水果）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “Instead of mostly eating fruit from trees, they began to include grasses and possibly meat”，明确提到肉类和草类取代了水果，因此答案为 “fruit”。
题目翻译：饮食习性 - 露西附近发现的_\___\___表明这也是她的食物
答案：eggs（卵）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “Fossilised crocodile and turtle eggs were found near her skeleton, suggesting that Lucy died while foraging for them”，明确提到附近发现的卵是她的食物，因此答案为 “eggs”。
题目翻译：饮食习性 - 发现的_\___\___上有痕迹，可能由取食工具造成
答案：bones（骨骼）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “in 2010 archaeologists uncovered animal bones with scratches that seem to have been made by stone tools”，明确提到带有痕迹的动物骨骼，因此答案为 “bones”。
题目翻译：与现代人对比 - 现代人的_\___\___比露西长
答案：childhood（童年）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “Lucy’s childhood was much briefer than ours”，明确提到现代人童年比露西长，因此答案为 “childhood”。
题目翻译：与现代人对比 - 现代人的_\___\___发育似乎比露西晚
答案：brains（大脑）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “a recent study of a 3-year-old early human suggested that their brains matured much earlier than ours do”，明确提到现代人的大脑发育比露西晚，因此答案为 “brains”。

items.text:
（1）题目 6：Movement - upright movement possibly started among the \___\____ of trees
题目翻译：移动方式 - 直立行走可能起源于树的_\___\___之间
答案：branches（树枝）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “It may be that upright walking evolved in the trees, as a way to walk along branches that would otherwise be too flexible”，明确提到直立行走可能起源于在树枝间行走，因此答案为 “branches”。

（2）题目 7：Movement - probably moved to the \___\____ in search of food
题目翻译：移动方式 - 可能为寻找食物走向_\___\___
答案：ground（地面）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “hunting for food may have been the real reason for heading to the ground”，明确提到为寻找食物走向地面，因此答案为 “ground”。

（3）题目 8：Diet and eating habits - analysis of food in the \___\____ of the skeletons of early humans shows changes in their diet
题目翻译：饮食习性 - 对早期人类骨骼_\___\___中食物的分析显示其饮食变化
答案：teeth（牙齿）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “Studies of the remains of food trapped on preserved human teeth indicate that several species, including Lucy’s, were expanding their diet”，明确提到分析牙齿上的食物残留，因此答案为 “teeth”。

（4）题目 9：Diet and eating habits - it is likely that meat and grasses were substituted for \___\____
题目翻译：饮食习性 - 肉类和草类可能取代了_\___\___
答案：fruit（水果）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “Instead of mostly eating fruit from trees, they began to include grasses and possibly meat”，明确提到肉类和草类取代了水果，因此答案为 “fruit”。

（5）题目 10：Diet and eating habits - \___\____ that were located close to Lucy suggest these were also part of her diet
题目翻译：饮食习性 - 露西附近发现的_\___\___表明这也是她的食物
答案：eggs（卵）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “Fossilised crocodile and turtle eggs were found near her skeleton, suggesting that Lucy died while foraging for them”，明确提到附近发现的卵是她的食物，因此答案为 “eggs”。

（6）题目 11：Diet and eating habits - \___\____ that were found had marks on them, possibly made by tools used for eating
题目翻译：饮食习性 - 发现的_\___\___上有痕迹，可能由取食工具造成
答案：bones（骨骼）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “in 2010 archaeologists uncovered animal bones with scratches that seem to have been made by stone tools”，明确提到带有痕迹的动物骨骼，因此答案为 “bones”。

（7）题目 12：Comparisons with modern-day humans - modern-day humans have a longer \___\____ than Lucy did
题目翻译：与现代人对比 - 现代人的_\___\___比露西长
答案：childhood（童年）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “Lucy’s childhood was much briefer than ours”，明确提到现代人童年比露西长，因此答案为 “childhood”。

（8）题目 13：Comparisons with modern-day humans - the \___\____ of modern-day humans appear to develop later than Lucy’s did
题目翻译：与现代人对比 - 现代人的_\___\___发育似乎比露西晚
答案：brains（大脑）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “a recent study of a 3-year-old early human suggested that their brains matured much earlier than ours do”，明确提到现代人的大脑发育比露西晚，因此答案为 “brains”。
